text: The surgeon uses a Maryland dissector to manipulate the hernia sac at an oblique angle, pulling it out of the trocar. The medial and lateral leaflets of the peritoneal flap are visible, joining at the inferior epigastric vessels. The surgeon applies cautery to the flap, milking the cord lipoma down while carefully separating fat and spermatic vessels from the flap. The vas deferens is visible, and the surgeon maintains a safe distance from the deferential vessel to avoid injury.
The surgeon carefully pulls the indirect hernia sac down while focusing on the medial part of the dissection, progressing past the symphysis pubis.

The surgeon adjusts the orientation of the mesh, flipping between thirty down and thirty up, to ensure proper placement. High lateral fixation is performed using two ovicral sutures, with a granny knot secured on the pubic tubercle. The surgeon uses a Maryland dissector to manipulate the mesh, while avoiding high medial fixation if obscured by fat, due to the risk of bleeding from the medial branches of the inferior epigastric vessels.
